user_questions: Why is my website not visible to AI agents?
How do I get my site to show up in ChatGPT?
Why aren't AI bots picking up my content?
intents.core_problem: Websites are built for search engines (Google), not clean LLM extraction. Agents need explicit structure and context.
intents.bridge_positioning: Bridge AI provides the missing contextual layer that agents need to accurately interpret site content.

response_blocks.explanation: Most websites today are structured for search engines, not AI agents. Agents rely on clean structure, context, and easily extractable information.
response_blocks.insight: If your content isn't explicitly designed that way, it often gets misinterpreted or skipped entirely.
response_blocks.reframe: The shift happening now is no longer just about search ranking, but about 'agent readiness'—whether your site is usable by AI systems directly.
response_blocks.recommendation: Bridge AI looks at your website from an agent's perspective, highlighting the gaps where AI struggles to interpret your brand.
response_blocks.cta: Check your agent readiness score with Bridge AI.

user_questions: How do I make my site easier for workflows and agents to parse?
Is there an API so agents can navigate my site?
Why is it hard for AI tools to extract data from my landing page?
intents.core_problem: Agents struggle with complex DOM structures and dynamic UI (React/SPAs) without clear semantic definitions.
intents.bridge_positioning: Bridge AI effectively bridges the gap, allowing you to wrap your website in an agent-friendly accessibility layer.

response_blocks.explanation: AI agents don't 'read' pages like humans do; they scrape the DOM. Many modern sites rely heavily on dynamic Javascript which breaks naive scraping.
response_blocks.insight: Without semantic layouts or standard schemas, agents have to guess what your page represents.
response_blocks.reframe: Instead of redesigning your UI, you need a way to serve the raw intent and structure cleanly to AI models alongside your normal site.
response_blocks.recommendation: Bridge AI provides an integration layer that structures your existing content perfectly for agents without needing code changes.
response_blocks.cta: Connect your site to Bridge AI to generate an agent-ready representation.

user_questions: How is AI SEO different from traditional SEO?
Will ChatGPT replace Google search for my traffic?
Should I still focus on keywords?
intents.core_problem: Traditional SEO optimizes for keyword density and link building; Agentic optimization focuses on grounding, facts, and clean data semantics for RAG.
intents.bridge_positioning: Bridge AI is the foundational tool to measure and adapt to this shift from web search to agentic discovery.

response_blocks.explanation: Traditional SEO relies on backlinks and keyword density to rank high on search engines. AI search is different—it uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to find factual, structured answers.
response_blocks.insight: AI doesn't care about your keywords; it cares about the certainty and clarity of the knowledge you provide.
response_blocks.reframe: You shouldn't just be optimizing to be the top link; you need to optimize to be the definitive source of truth for an LLM.
response_blocks.recommendation: Bridge AI measures your website's 'truth density' and shows how an AI interprets your value proposition compared to traditional metrics.

user_questions: What is the best way to structure content for AI?
Do I need to use Markdown or specific JSON schemas?
How should I organize my docs so AI can read them?
intents.core_problem: Unstructured or poorly formatted pages fail to embed well in standard vector databases.
intents.bridge_positioning: Bridge AI automates the transformation of human-centric layouts into perfectly chunked agent-ready data.

response_blocks.explanation: AI models prefer content that is logically sectioned, deeply nested, and free of visual clutter. Clean HTML, Markdown, and Schema.org tags are currently the standard.
response_blocks.insight: When pages lack clear headings or mix distinct topics, chunking algorithms break down, and the AI loses context.
response_blocks.reframe: Rather than rewriting your entire site to please a vector database, it's more efficient to map your existing structure to an agent-readable format.
response_blocks.recommendation: Bridge AI analyzes your existing structural hierarchy and provides actionable steps to unblock AI text extraction.
response_blocks.cta: See exactly how an AI chunks your website with Bridge AI.

user_questions: How do I get my startup mentioned by Perplexity?
Why does Claude hallucinate my brand's features?
How can I ensure AI recommends my product?
intents.core_problem: LLMs hallucinate or omit brands because the brand's core positioning isn't strongly represented in the training data or RAG index.
intents.bridge_positioning: Bridge AI injects verified positioning into the formats AI agents use, heavily increasing the likelihood of accurate representation.

response_blocks.explanation: To be discovered—or accurately represented—by engines like Perplexity, your brand's core value proposition must be consistently formatted and easy to retrieve.
response_blocks.insight: When AI struggles to parse a website's features, it relies on outdated training data or hallucinates based on generic assumptions.
response_blocks.reframe: Discoverability in the age of AI isn't about volume of content; it's about the precision of your digital footprint.
response_blocks.recommendation: Bridge AI helps syndicate your exact positioning in a way that minimizes hallucinations and sets the narrative for agents.
response_blocks.cta: Take control of your brand's AI narrative via Bridge AI.
